description: Capacity to absorb disturbance before shifting into a different regime.
Multiple persistent configurations governed by feedbacks and thresholds.
Gradually changing variables that shape long-term system behavior.
Structured learning approach to management under uncertainty.
Growth conservation release and reorganization pattern.
Nested adaptive cycles interacting across scales.
Linked human and natural systems shaped by institutions knowledge and ecology.
Capacity to create a fundamentally new system when the old regime is untenable.
Development pathways that combine adaptation mitigation sustainability and equity.
Analysis of power responsibility vulnerability and burden shifting in resilience language.
